---
title: "Pink Milkwort (Senega incarnata)"
slug: "senega-incarnata"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/476400117/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Pink Milkwort"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Fabales"
tax_family: "Polygalaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Fabales", "Polygalaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/476400117/medium.jpg" alt="Pink Milkwort" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/lmcconachie">
lmcconachie</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/265243393">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Fabales
-   **Family:** Polygalaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=1569114)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet, sandy habitats.

## Natural history notes

A small wildflower characterized by its slender stems, narrow leaves, and clusters of bright pink to rose-colored flowers. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet, sandy habitats such as flatwoods, wet prairies, and seepage areas. It grows during the warmer months and provides a nectar source for small pollinators, often occurring as a subtle component of native groundcover vegetation.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/1569114-Senega-incarnata)
-   [Local profile](https://fsus.ncbg.unc.edu/show-taxon-detail.php?taxonid=3034)
